subject: civic
learning_objectives: State the goals of citizenship education
Identify and explain types of law

sections.heading: Meaning of Citizenship
sections.content: A citizen is a person recognised by law as a member of a community, nation, or state. Citizenship means membership of a nation or state.

sections.heading: Meaning of Citizenship Education
sections.content: Citizenship education is the art of creating social, political, and economic consciousness in the citizens.

sections.heading: Goals of Citizenship Education
sections.content:
a. To establish national consciousness and national unity in the citizens of the country.
b. To make good citizens of the country.
c. To unite the citizens. As the citizens are taught the same things, the dangers of disunity will be reduced.
d. To help citizens to know their rights, duties and obligations, national values and ethics, thereby creating a sense of belonging in them.
e. To educate citizens on their expected roles in the society and as leaders of the country.

sections.heading: Meaning of Law
sections.content: A law is a system of rules that people in a particular country or area must obey. There are different types of laws.

sections.heading: Types of Law
sections.content:
a. Public Law: These laws concern the state, either in international relations, or in the relationship between the state and the individual.

b. Criminal Law: A law that concerns crimes and their punishments. A crime is an illegal action which can be punished by law.

c. Civil Law: It provides rules for the conduct of relations and transactions between persons or organisations.

d. Administrative Law: A law used to review the decisions of government agencies.

e. International Law: The law that governs affairs between countries.

Others include:
f. Private Law
g. Aviation Law
h. Education Law
i. Entertainment Law or Media Law
j. Maritime Law
